hints: We use "must" to express strong necessity or desire.

a1.must: "Must see" expresses a strong desire or necessity to watch a movie.

hints:
We use "can't" to express impossibility to do something.
We use "must" to express obligation or necessity.

a1.can't: "Can't" shows that I can't do it this weekend.

a2.must: "Must go" expresses the obligation to go to Ipswich for the birthday.

hints: We use "will" to express a spontaneous decision.

a1.will: "Will call" expresses a spontaneous decision to call David.

hints: We use "should" for advice or recommendation.

a1.should: "Should call" gives advice to call immediately.

hints:
We use "can" to express possibility.
We use "would you mind" for a very polite request.

a1.can: "Can talk" expresses the possibility to talk to him tonight.

a2.would: "Would you mind" is a very polite way to ask for something.

hints:
We use "will" for a promise or certainty about a future action.
We use "won't" in tag questions for confirmation.
In tag questions after the negative "won't" we put the positive "will".

a1.will: Tag question with "will you?" after "won't eat" seeks confirmation.

a2.won't: "Won't eat" in tag question seeks confirmation that he won't eat the cookie.
